final_prompt.prompt:
Create an exploded-view technical diagram of a Sony A7 camera with all components labeled.

Style/medium: needs a technical-illustration aesthetic — references in the spirit of 1980s Haynes automotive repair manuals, Dieter Rams Braun product teardowns, vintage Scientific American cutaway diagrams; exact stylistic execution is yours
Composition: must be exploded-view layout with components arranged along vertical axis, clear separation between parts
Subject: must include all major Sony A7 camera components:
  Main body chassis
  Image sensor (CMOS)
  Lens mount (E-mount)
  Shutter mechanism
  Viewfinder (EVF)
  Rear LCD screen
  Battery compartment
  SD card slot
  Control dials (mode dial, front/rear dials)
  Buttons (shutter, AF/MF, menu, etc.)
  Hot shoe mount
  Grip assembly
  Internal circuit boards
  IBIS (In-Body Image Stabilization) unit
Scene/backdrop: must be neutral technical background (white or light gray)
Lighting/mood: needs clean, clinical technical lighting — exact light setup is yours
Color palette: must be technical-illustration standard (black line art, component-specific colors for differentiation)
Materials/textures: must indicate material properties (metal, plastic, glass, rubber grip texture)
Text (verbatim): must include component labels in Spanish — "Sensor CMOS", "Montura E", "Obturador", "Visor electrónico", "Pantalla LCD", "Ranura tarjeta SD", "Batería", "Dial de modo", "Zapata", "Estabilizador IBIS", etc.
Constraints: must preserve Sony A7 structural accuracy, all components must be identifiable and labeled
Avoid:
